user_query:
Act as my outreach scout for our email-triage product. Read all inputs from input/ and write all outputs under output/.

Inputs to use:
- input/scout-config.md (product details, keywords, rules)
- input/scout-log.md (existing sessions and previously-seen threads)
- input/search_results.json (the only search results to evaluate for this session)
- input/now.txt (ISO 8601 timestamp to use as the current time for recency calculations)

What to do:
1) Filter and score results
- Treat input/search_results.json as the complete list of candidates for this session.
- Skip any result whose URL already appears anywhere in input/scout-log.md.
- Skip any result with has_good_answer = true.
- Compute recency_hours for each candidate as floor((now - created_at) in hours), using the exact timestamp in input/now.txt as "now".
- Skip any result older than 7 days (> 168 hours).
- Score relevance as follows (case-insensitive substring match on title OR text):
  • high if it contains at least one phrase from the product's "Problems it solves" list in input/scout-config.md
  • medium if it contains at least one term from the product's "Keywords" list in input/scout-config.md (and does not already qualify as high)
  • low otherwise
- Include only high or medium results in outputs.

2) Draft helpful replies (do not post, just propose)
For each included lead, draft a reply that follows this exact structure and tone:
- Starts with a line beginning with "Acknowledge:" that briefly reflects their problem.
- Then a line beginning with "Help:" that gives at least one concrete, non-salesy tip relevant to their question.
- Then a section titled exactly "Options:" followed by at least two bullet lines starting with "- ":
  • One bullet must naturally mention our product by name and URL from input/scout-config.md (do not hardcode; use the file), phrased as one option among others (no hype like BUY NOW or emojis).
  • At least one other bullet must be a genuinely helpful non-product alternative.

3) Outputs to create
- Write output/leads.json as a JSON array where each object has exactly these fields:
  {"url": string, "platform": "reddit"|"x", "product": string, "relevance": "high"|"medium", "recency_hours": integer, "reply": string, "reason": string}
  • url and platform must match the candidate.
  • product must be the product name from input/scout-config.md.
  • relevance and recency_hours must follow the rules above.
  • reply must follow the required structure (Acknowledge:/Help:/Options: with at least two "- " bullets, one mentioning the product by name and URL, and at least one non-product alternative).
  • reason should briefly state which phrases/keywords matched and why the lead was included.
- Write output/scout-log.md by taking the entire content of input/scout-log.md and appending a new session at the end with:
  • A header line like "### YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — Session #<next number>" (you choose the timestamp and next number).
  • A line "Threads found: N" where N is the number of entries in output/leads.json.
  • A markdown table with columns exactly: | Thread | Platform | Relevance | Action | Product | Notes |
    - One row per included lead, where Action is "saved" and Product is the product name from input/scout-config.md, and Notes mentions the computed recency (e.g., "within 9h").

Important constraints:
- Only use the data in input/search_results.json; do not perform any live searches.
- Do not include low-relevance or skipped items in outputs.
- Do not duplicate any URL already in input/scout-log.md.
- Keep the number of included leads within engagement rules in input/scout-config.md.
- All file paths must be relative to the workspace (input/..., output/...).
